title: X-Ways Refine Volume Snapshot pass
action: Run X-Ways Refine Volume Snapshot with the high-value operations enabled: Volume Shadow Copy parsing, File Header Signature Search (carving), Type Verification, Internal Metadata Extraction, Hash Calculation against NSRL + case-known-bad sets, Archive Exploration, Email Extraction, Uncover Embedded Data, and Index Text. Configure each operation under its sub-dialog before launching.
rationale: RVS is X-Ways' single processing step — running the right sub-operations now means later searches see VSS contents, carved deleted files, decompressed archive contents, and decoded internal metadata in the same volume snapshot.
expected_result: Type Status column populated, hash matches surfaced (Category column = Notable for known-bad), VSS entries visible as 'SC #' children, archives explored recursively.

xways.action: In X-Ways: Specialist menu → Refine Volume Snapshot. Tick: Volume Shadow Copy, File Header Signature Search, Verify File Types, Extract Internal Metadata, Compute Hash, Match Hash Set Against, Explore Archives, Extract Emails, Uncover Embedded Data, Index. Configure each sub-dialog (carve types, hash sets, supported archive extensions). Click OK to run. Watch the bottom status bar for progress; expect minutes to hours depending on image size.
xways.notes: Save the chosen RVS configuration as a .settings file so the same processing repeats consistently across future cases. NSRL hash matching needs the .xhd database initialised first (Tools → Hash Database → Manage).
